question:
The following data were drawn one observation at a time in the order records: gbggbgbgbggbgbg.
where $b$ denotes a defective item and $g$ denotes a good item. The experiment was performed to test the following hypothesis:

$$
\mathrm{H}_0: \mathrm{p}=\mathrm{p}_0=0.10 \text { versus } \mathrm{H}_1: \mathrm{p}=\mathrm{p}_1=0.2
$$

where $p$ denotes the proportion defective items in the population. It is desired to reject $\mathrm{H}_0$ when it is true with probability 0.05 and to accept $\mathrm{H}_0$ when $\mathrm{H}_1$ is true with probability 0.20 . Using the sequential testing plan, we wish to determine whether we would accept or reject a lot on the basis of the observations above.
data:
$$
\mathrm{s}_{\mathrm{b}} n+b_1<d_n<\mathrm{s}_{\mathrm{b}} n+b_0
$$
\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 3.13 The sampling procedure}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline $n$ & $x_n$ & $d_n$ & Acceptance \# & Rejection \# \\
\hline 1 & 0 & 0 & - & - \\
\hline 2 & 1 & 1 & - & - \\
\hline 3 & 0 & 1 & - & - \\
\hline 4 & 0 & 1 & - & - \\
\hline 5 & 1 & 2 & - & 5 \\
\hline 6 & 0 & 2 & - & 5 \\
\hline 7 & 1 & 3 & - & 5 \\
\hline 8 & 0 & 3 & - & 5 \\
\hline 9 & 1 & 4 & - & 5 \\
\hline 10 & 0 & 4 & - & 5 \\
\hline 11 & 0 & 4 & - & 6 \\
\hline 12 & 1 & 5 & - & 6 \\
\hline 13 & 0 & 5 & - & 6 \\
\hline 14 & 1 & 6 & 0 & 6 \\
\hline 15 & 0 & 6 & 0 & 6 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
answer:
Given $p_0=0.10, p_1=0.20, \alpha=0.05, \beta=0.20$. From Eq. (3.88) we have

$$
\begin{gathered}
s_b=-\frac{\ln \left(\frac{1-p_1}{1-p_0}\right)}{\ln \left(\frac{p_1\left(1-p_0\right)}{p_0\left(1-p_1\right)}\right)}=-\frac{\ln \left(\frac{1-0.20}{1-0.10}\right)}{\ln \left(\frac{0.2(1-0.10)}{0.10(1-0.20)}\right)}=0.1452 \\
b_1=\frac{\ln \left(\frac{\beta}{1-\alpha}\right)}{\ln \left(\frac{p_1\left(1-p_0\right)}{p_0\left(1-p_1\right)}\right)}=\frac{\ln \left(\frac{0.2}{1-0.05}\right)}{\ln \left(\frac{0.2(1-0.1)}{0.1(1-0.2)}\right)}=-1.9214, \\
b_0=\frac{\ln \left(\frac{1-\beta}{\alpha}\right)}{\ln \left(\frac{p_1\left(1-p_0\right)}{p_0\left(1-p_1\right)}\right)}=\frac{\ln \left(\frac{1-0.2}{0.05}\right)}{\ln \left(\frac{0.2(1-0.1)}{0.1(1-0.2)}\right)}=3.4190,
\end{gathered}
$$


Thus,

$$
s_b n+b_1<d_n<s_b n+b_0
$$
$$
0.1452 n-1.9214<d_n<0.1452 n+3.4190
$$


The acceptance line is: $0.1452 n-1.9214$.
The rejection line is: $0.1452 n+3.4190$.
From Table 3.13, the cumulative number of defective units $\mathrm{d}_{14}=6$ falls above the rejection line when $n=14$. Therefore, the sampling is stopped at the 14th observation and that the lot would be rejected. That is, reject the hypothesis $\mathrm{H}_0: \mathrm{p}=0.10$.
